name: French Toast Cheddar Sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
1. Set out a heavy skillet or cast iron griddle.
2. Beat the eggs slightly in a pie tin or shallow bowl and add the milk or cream and salt. Mix well.
3. Spread four slices of bread on a flat working surface. Spread one side with prepared mustard and place a slice of cheddar cheese on each.
4. Butter the remaining four slices of bread and top each cheese slice with bread, butter side down.
5. Heat the butter in the skillet or on the griddle until melted.
6. Carefully dip each sandwich into the egg mixture, coating both sides. Allow excess to drain back into the bowl.
7. Dip only as many sandwiches as will lie flat in the skillet without overcrowding.
8. Cook over low heat until browned. Turn and cook the other sides.
9. Repeat with remaining sandwiches. Add more butter if needed to prevent sticking.
10. Alternatively, place the sandwiches on a greased baking sheet and bake at 450°F for 8-10 minutes.
11. Serve hot.
